android.permission.WRITE_CONTACTS.full_description:
Allows the app to modify the data about your contacts stored on your phone.
      This permission allows apps to delete contact data.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.short_description: access precise location only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.full_description: This app can get your exact location only when it is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them. This may increase battery consumption.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.short_description: read the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows the app to read the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.full_description:
Allows the app to
        have itself started as soon as the system has finished booting.
        This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the
        app to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.READ_CONTACTS.full_description:
Allows the app to read data about your contacts stored on your phone.
      This permission allows apps to save your contact data, and malicious apps may share contact data without your knowledge.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view information
      about Wi-Fi networking, such as whether Wi-Fi is enabled and name of
      connected Wi-Fi devices.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.short_description: access approximate location (network-based) only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.full_description: This app can get your location based on network sources such as cell towers and Wi-Fi networks, but only when the app is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows the app to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.GET_TASKS.full_description:
Allows the app to retrieve information
       about currently and recently running tasks.  This may allow the app to
       discover information about which applications are used on the device.

android.permission.CAMERA.full_description: This app can take pictures and record videos using the camera at any time.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description:
Allows the app to create
     network sockets and use custom network protocols. The browser and other
     applications provide means to send data to the internet, so this
     permission is not required to send data to the internet.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view
      information about network connections such as which networks exist and are
      connected.

android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE.full_description: Allows the app to make use of foreground services.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.short_description: modify or delete the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows the app to write the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows an application to read from external storage

android.permission.READ_CONTACTS.full_description: Allows an application to read all of the contact (address) data stored on your phone. Malicious applications can use this to send your data to other people.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the information about the status of Wi-Fi.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.short_description: coarse (network-based) location
android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.full_description: Access coarse location sources, such as the mobile network database, to determine an approximate phone location, where available. Malicious applications can use this to determine approximately where you are.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows an application to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the status of all networks.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The binary exhibits several high-confidence indicators of malicious intent, specifically focusing on persistence and privilege escalation. Key behaviors include: 1) Persistence: The sample constructs and executes a command string using 'schtasks' to create a scheduled task with the '/sc ONLOGON' trigger, ensuring the binary runs every time a user logs in. 2) Privilege Escalation: The scheduled task is configured with '/ru System', attempting to execute the payload with SYSTEM-level privileges. 3) Obfuscation: Function sub_401000 performs custom string decryption/de-obfuscation using XOR and modulus operations on internal data arrays to hide its commands and API names. 4) Dynamic Resolution: It dynamically loads ntdll.dll and user32.dll to resolve sensitive functions, bypassing static import analysis. 5) Anti-Analysis: Subroutine sub_401ed8 performs environment validation by creating and manipulating a temporary file named 'jhuhugit.temp'.

Allows the app to
        have itself started as soon as the system has finished booting.
        This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the
        app to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.READ_CONTACTS.full_description:
Allows the app to read data about your contacts stored on your phone.
      This permission allows apps to save your contact data, and malicious apps may share contact data without your knowledge.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view
      information about network connections such as which networks exist and are
      connected.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.short_description: access approximate location (network-based) only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.full_description: This app can get your location based on network sources such as cell towers and Wi-Fi networks, but only when the app is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows the app to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.GET_TASKS.full_description:
Allows the app to retrieve information
       about currently and recently running tasks.  This may allow the app to
       discover information about which applications are used on the device.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view information
      about Wi-Fi networking, such as whether Wi-Fi is enabled and name of
      connected Wi-Fi devices.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description:
Allows the app to create
     network sockets and use custom network protocols. The browser and other
     applications provide means to send data to the internet, so this
     permission is not required to send data to the internet.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.short_description: access precise location only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.full_description: This app can get your exact location only when it is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them. This may increase battery consumption.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.short_description: modify or delete the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows the app to write the contents of your shared storage.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a Windows program executable started from a suspicious folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Bad Opsec Defaults Sacrificial Processes With Improper Arguments
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects attackers using tooling with bad opsec defaults.
E.g. spawning a sacrificial process to inject a capability into the process without taking into account how the process is normally run.
One trivial example of this is using rundll32.exe without arguments as a sacrificial process (default in CS, now highlighted by c2lint), running WerFault without arguments (Kraken - credit am0nsec), and other examples.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Oleg Kolesnikov @securonix invrep_de, oscd.community, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious start of rundll32.exe without any parameters as found in CobaltStrike beacon activity

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: LOLBAS rundll32 without expected arguments (via cmdline)
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects use of rundll32 as a LOLBAS binary where rundll32 is passed unexpected arguments such as a .iso instead of .dll (i.e. rundll32.exe test.iso, evilexport).

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Use Short Name Path in Command Line
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid command-line detection

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Use Short Name Path in Image
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image detection

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a "regsvr32" execution where the DLL doesn't contain a common file extension.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Rundll32 Execution With Uncommon DLL Extension
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the execution of rundll32 with a command line that doesn't contain a common extension
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Tim Shelton, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Yassine Oukessou

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects modification of the registry settings used for Internet Explorer and other Windows components that use these settings. An attacker can abuse this registry key to add a domain to the trusted sites Zone or insert javascript for persistence
